(born 9 February 1937) is an English actor and television presenter. He played [David Barlow](/wiki/David_Barlow_(Coronation_Street) "David Barlow (Coronation Street)") in the ITV soap opera *[Coronation Street](/wiki/Coronation_Street "Coronation Street")* as a regular from 1960 to 1961, and again from 1963 to 1968, and Nicholas Black in *[Brookside](/wiki/Brookside_(TV_series) "Brookside (TV series)")* between 1985 and 1986. His other acting credits include playing Mike in *[Top Secret](/wiki/Top_Secret_(TV_series) "Top Secret (TV series)")* (1961–1962), a recurring role in *[Heartbeat](/wiki/Heartbeat_(British_TV_series) "Heartbeat (British TV series)")* (1994–1995), and various roles in *[Doctors](/wiki/Doctors_(2000_TV_series) "Doctors (2000 TV series)")* (2004–2016). He also presented the children's television series *Picture Box* and *[Hickory House](/wiki/Hickory_House_(TV_series) "Hickory House (TV series)")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English actor and television presenter (born 1937)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article includes a list of [general references](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources#General_references "Wikipedia:Citing sources"), but **it lacks sufficient corresponding [inline citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources#Inline_citations "Wikipedia:Citing sources")**. Please help to [improve](/wiki/Wikipedia:WikiProject_Reliability "Wikipedia:WikiProject Reliability") this article by [introducing](/wiki/Wikipedia:When_to_cite "Wikipedia:When to cite") more precise citations. *(March 2013)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Alan Rothwell | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1937-02-09) 9 February 1937 (age 89)  [Oldham](/wiki/Oldham "Oldham"), [Lancashire](/wiki/Lancashire "Lancashire"), England |
| Notable work | * *[Coronation Street](/wiki/Coronation_Street "Coronation Street")* * *[Top Secret](/wiki/Top_Secret_(TV_series) "Top Secret (TV series)")* * *[Brookside](/wiki/Brookside_(TV_series) "Brookside (TV series)")* |
| Spouse(s) | Marjorie Ward (divorced)  Mollie Shanti (divorced) |
| Children | 2 |

**Alan Rothwell** (born 9 February 1937) is an English actor and television presenter. He played [David Barlow](/wiki/David_Barlow_(Coronation_Street) "David Barlow (Coronation Street)") in the ITV soap opera *[Coronation Street](/wiki/Coronation_Street "Coronation Street")* as a regular from 1960 to 1961, and again from 1963 to 1968, and Nicholas Black in *[Brookside](/wiki/Brookside_(TV_series) "Brookside (TV series)")* between 1985 and 1986. His other acting credits include playing Mike in *[Top Secret](/wiki/Top_Secret_(TV_series) "Top Secret (TV series)")* (1961–1962), a recurring role in *[Heartbeat](/wiki/Heartbeat_(British_TV_series) "Heartbeat (British TV series)")* (1994–1995), and various roles in *[Doctors](/wiki/Doctors_(2000_TV_series) "Doctors (2000 TV series)")* (2004–2016). He also presented the children's television series *Picture Box* and *[Hickory House](/wiki/Hickory_House_(TV_series) "Hickory House (TV series)")*.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alan_Rothwell&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Rothwell was born in [Oldham](/wiki/Oldham "Oldham"), [Lancashire](/wiki/Lancashire "Lancashire"). He first came to fame playing the character Jimmy Grange in *[The Archers](/wiki/The_Archers "The Archers")*, then [David Barlow](/wiki/David_Barlow_(Coronation_Street) "David Barlow (Coronation Street)") in the then new [ITV](/wiki/ITV_(TV_network) "ITV (TV network)") [soap opera](/wiki/Soap_opera "Soap opera") *[Coronation Street](/wiki/Coronation_Street "Coronation Street")* as a regular from December 1960 until June 1961, then appeared for two episodes in June 1963, before returning as a regular from December 1964 to April 1968. The character was killed off off-screen two years later. He also featured as a regular character in all 26 episodes of the 1961–1962 British spy series [*Top Secret*](/wiki/Top_Secret_(TV_series) "Top Secret (TV series)") in the role of Mike. He was a guest star in *[Gideon's Way](/wiki/Gideon%27s_Way "Gideon's Way")* 1964 as a young man wrongly accused of killing his girlfriend played by [Carol White](/wiki/Carol_White "Carol White"). He also had roles in the rediscovered 1960 film *[Linda](/wiki/Linda_(1960_film) "Linda (1960 film)")*, also starring Carol White, and the 1971 film *[Zeppelin](/wiki/Zeppelin_(film) "Zeppelin (film)")*, starring [Michael York](/wiki/Michael_York "Michael York") and [Elke Sommer](/wiki/Elke_Sommer "Elke Sommer").

Rothwell then became known as a presenter to a generation of children, appearing on the children's television programmes *Picture Box* from 1969 to 1989 and *[Hickory House](/wiki/Hickory_House_(TV_series) "Hickory House (TV series)")* from 1973 to 1978.

Rothwell returned to soap operas in 1985, this time as the heroin addict [Nicholas Black](/wiki/Nicholas_Black "Nicholas Black") in *[Brookside](/wiki/Brookside_(TV_series) "Brookside (TV series)")*. In 2002, he appeared in the television drama film *[Shipman](/wiki/Shipman_(film) "Shipman (film)")*. He also played Gerry Stringer in all six episodes of *[Dead Man Weds](/wiki/Dead_Man_Weds "Dead Man Weds")* in 2005. Among his many other television credits are parts in *[Casualty](/wiki/Casualty_(TV_series) "Casualty (TV series)")*, *[Emmerdale](/wiki/Emmerdale "Emmerdale")*, *[Heartbeat](/wiki/Heartbeat_(British_TV_series) "Heartbeat (British TV series)")*, *[Doctors](/wiki/Doctors_(2000_TV_series) "Doctors (2000 TV series)")*, *[Shameless](/wiki/Shameless_(British_TV_series) "Shameless (British TV series)")* and *[Bedlam](/wiki/Bedlam_(2011_TV_series) "Bedlam (2011 TV series)")*, *A Song For Jenny, (BBC 2015)*, *[Rovers](/wiki/Rovers_(British_TV_series) "Rovers (British TV series)"), (Jellylegs, 2016)*. In 2004, Rothwell guest-starred in the *[Doctor Who](/wiki/Doctor_Who "Doctor Who")* audio adventure *[The Twilight Kingdom](/wiki/The_Twilight_Kingdom "The Twilight Kingdom")*.

In 2015, Rothwell appeared as a villager in the BBC TV series *[The Musketeers](/wiki/The_Musketeers "The Musketeers")* episode 2.5 "The Return".

In 2018, Rothwell appeared as Douglas in 20th Century-Fox's British comedy, *[Walk Like A Panther](/wiki/Walk_like_a_Panther_(film) "Walk like a Panther (film)")*, released in cinemas 9 March.
